---
title: "Models"
output: pdf_document
date: "2023-12-05"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r}
#set up libraries
library(caret)
library(ggplot2)
library('naivebayes')
library('C50')
library(Metrics)
library('tinytex')

#Open file
# Set your working directory to the folder containing NBAPlayerData.csv
# setwd("path/to/your/data")
nbadf <- read.csv('NBAPlayerData.csv')
```



##create training and testing datasets

```{r}
sample_size <- floor(0.8*nrow(nbadf))
#check size
sample_size

# Identify constant variables
constant_vars <- sapply(nbadf, function(x) length(unique(x)) == 1)

# Exclude constant variables
nbadf <- nbadf[, !constant_vars]

# get train data index
train_ind <- sample(seq_len(nrow(nbadf)), size = sample_size)
# generate training and test dataset
train <- nbadf[train_ind,]
test <- nbadf[-train_ind,]

#remove empty values
train <- train[complete.cases(train),]
test <- test[complete.cases(test),]

#set cross validation method: ## 5-fold CV , repeated two times
fitControl <- trainControl(method = "repeatedcv", number = 5, repeats = 2)
```



## model #1: gaussian kernel

```{r}
## svm with gaussian kernel
svmradial <- train(MP ~ X3P.+eFG.+TRB+AST+STL+BLK+PTS, data = train,
                   method = "svmRadial",
                   trControl = fitControl)
#apply model and evaluate prediction results for svm gaussian
prediction_svmgaussian <- predict(svmradial, newdata = test)
rmse(prediction_svmgaussian,test$MP)
```



## model #2: Linear Regression
```{r}
lm_model <- lm(MP ~ X3P. + eFG. + TRB + AST + STL + BLK + PTS, data = train)

# Extract summary
summary(lm_model)
```


##convert continuous minutes played values to labels by using a 75 percent threshold
```{r}
threshold <- quantile(nbadf$MP, 0.75)
nbadf$MP <- factor(with(nbadf, ifelse(nbadf$MP <= threshold, 'low', 'high')))

# now check number of smaples in each category
table(nbadf$MP)



##create training and testing datasets

sample_size <- floor(0.8*nrow(nbadf))
#check size
sample_size

# Identify constant variables
constant_vars <- sapply(nbadf, function(x) length(unique(x)) == 1)

# Exclude constant variables
nbadf <- nbadf[, !constant_vars]

# get train data index
train_ind <- sample(seq_len(nrow(nbadf)), size = sample_size)
# generate training and test dataset
train <- nbadf[train_ind,]
test <- nbadf[-train_ind,]

#remove empty values
train <- train[complete.cases(train),]
test <- test[complete.cases(test),]

#set cross validation method: ## 5-fold CV , repeated two times
fitControl <- trainControl(method = "repeatedcv", number = 5, repeats = 2)
```





##Model #3: Decision tree
```{r}
# Decision tree
svmradial2 <- train(MP ~ X3P.+eFG.+TRB+AST+STL+BLK+PTS, data = train,
                   method = "C5.0", trControl = fitControl)
#apply model and evaluate prediction results for decision tree
prediction_dectree <- predict(svmradial2, newdata = test)
confusionMatrix(prediction_dectree,test$MP)
```
